pairs.question: How many teams participated in the 2007 ICC Cricket World Cup, and how were they initially divided?
pairs.answer: The 2007 ICC Cricket World Cup featured 16 competing teams, which were initially divided into four groups. The two best-performing teams from each group advanced to the Super 8 stage.

pairs.question: What was the format of the Super 8 round in the 2007 ICC Cricket World Cup?
pairs.answer: In the Super 8 round, each team played six matches against teams from the other three groups (the top two teams from each of those groups). Teams did not face opponents from their own group in this stage.

pairs.question: Which teams reached the semi-finals of the 2007 ICC Cricket World Cup, and who won the tournament?
pairs.answer: Australia, New Zealand, Sri Lanka, and South Africa reached the semi-finals. Australia defeated Sri Lanka in the final to win their fourth World Cup title and their third consecutive championship.

pairs.question: What notable streak did Australia achieve during the 2007 ICC Cricket World Cup?
pairs.answer: Australia extended their unbeaten streak in World Cup matches to 29, a record dating back to 23 May 1999, during the group stage of the 1999 World Cup.

pairs.question: Which teams were considered pre-tournament favorites but failed to advance past the group stage in 2007?
pairs.answer: India and Pakistan were pre-tournament favorites but failed to make it past the group stage in the 2007 ICC Cricket World Cup.

pairs.question: How did Bangladesh and Ireland perform in the 2007 ICC Cricket World Cup despite being underdogs?
pairs.answer: Bangladesh, the second-lowest ranked ICC Full Member at the time, and Ireland, an ICC Associate Member and World Cup debutant, both advanced to the Super 8 stage. Ireland defeated Pakistan, and Bangladesh defeated India en route to this achievement.

pairs.question: What happened to Pakistan's coach during the 2007 ICC Cricket World Cup, and what was the outcome of the investigation?
pairs.answer: Pakistan's coach, Bob Woolmer, died the day after Pakistan was knocked out of the tournament. Initially, police announced the death as suspicious, but eight months later, an open verdict was returned, and the case was closed.

pairs.question: How much revenue did the ICC distribute to its members following the 2007 ICC Cricket World Cup?
pairs.answer: The ICC distributed surplus tournament revenues totaling US$239 million to its members after the 2007 ICC Cricket World Cup.
